page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, utilizing the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 1536.0 GB of memory, providing a ratio of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. No local storage or GPU hardware is bundled with this instance.

Benchmark results place this instance in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and software compilation. Memory subsystem performance is also in the top tier, with high read and write bandwidths alongside a top-tier PassMark memory score. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top tier, matching the static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance is average, and certain workloads like image processing and small-model prompt processing show weaker relative performance.

The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which represents a key architectural tradeoff. The high memory capacity combined with top-tier multi-core and database benchmark results makes this server suitable for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores powered by 2.7 GHz AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 1536.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB per core, and a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the r8gb.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations, though single-core execution and image processing are average to weak. This resource profile makes the instance highly efficient for memory-intensive applications, large databases, and concurrent software compilation, while requiring network-attached storage for persistent data.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 192 dedicated ARMv9 cores, 1536 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database benchmark performance.
